name: Lead water sensor (PbrR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects lead Pb(II) in water. The PbrR metal-responsive regulator gates a CRISPRi circuit driving sfGFP, in spore-forming Bacillus subtilis 168 for rugged, field-storable testing.

input.detects: Bioavailable inorganic lead in drinking water
input.operatingRange: PbrR sensors respond from sub-µM to µM Pb(II); aim below the 10 µg/L (~0.05 µM) WHO/EPA action level

sensing.sensorMechanism: PbrR (a MerR-family regulator) binds Pb(II) and activates the pbr promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: Pb(II) -> PbrR activates Ppbr -> transcribes an anti-reporter sgRNA -> dCas9 + sgRNA repress a constitutive sfGFP cassette (CRISPRi) -> fluorescence inverts with lead. Pair a second inverter for turn-on output.

parts.name: PbrR regulator
parts.notes: Lead-responsive MerR-family activator; may require codon-optimisation for B. subtilis.

parts.name: dCas9
parts.source: Peters et al. 2016 (CRISPRi in B. subtilis)
parts.notes: CRISPRi is validated in B. subtilis.

output.result: Fluorescence change scales with lead concentration.

performance.notes: PbrR lead sensors and B. subtilis CRISPRi are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. Spores allow ambient-temperature storage and reconstitution in the field.

safety.biocontainment: B. subtilis is contained via auxotrophy/spore control; GRAS host.
safety.notes: GRAS, spore-forming chassis well suited to low-resource field deployment.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-reporter sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the sfGFP promoter; check B. subtilis off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: PbrR. TU2: Ppbr -> sgRNA. TU3: dCas9 + Pveg -> sfGFP. Integrate at amyE/thrC loci.

buildSteps.title: Transform B. subtilis 168
buildSteps.description: Use natural competence; select integrants; confirm low background.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Reconstitute spores, add water sample + lead standard curve.

buildSteps.title: Measure fluorescence
buildSteps.description: Interpolate Pb(II) from the standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining a PbrR lead-sensing module with B. subtilis CRISPRi
partsSources: Borremans et al. 2001 (pbr lead resistance/sensing, C. metallidurans)
Peters et al. 2016, Cell (CRISPRi in B. subtilis)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
